claim.content: Within Landau Fermi-liquid theory for electronic quasi-particles, let $Z$ denote the quasi-particle spectral weight (the magnitude of the discontinuity in the momentum occupation $n(\mathbf{k})$ at the Fermi vector $k_{\mathrm{F}}$) and let $m^{*}$ and $m$ denote the renormalized and the non-interacting effective masses respectively; then, for a band on which the quasi-particle energy is uniformly scaled relative to a non-interacting reference, the observed relation $E_{\mathrm{exp}}(\mathbf{k})\approx Z\,E_{\mathrm{DFT}}(\mathbf{k})$ implies an effective mass enhancement $m^{*}/m\approx 1/Z$, so that an observed energy renormalization factor $Z\approx 0.5$ corresponds to $m^{*}/m\approx 2$ within the Fermi-liquid framework.
